statement.markdown: You are given a sorted array of integers `nums` and an integer `target` to search for in the array. Implement a binary search algorithm to find the index of the target number in the array, or return -1 if it's not present.
statement.inputFormat: The first line will contain an integer n, the size of the array, followed by the elements of the array in sorted order. The second line will contain an integer target, the number to be searched in the array.
statement.outputFormat: The index of the target number in the array if present, -1 otherwise.
statement.notes: Time complexity should be O(log n), space complexity should be O(1).

examples.explanation: The target number 3 is present at index 2 in the array.
The target number 6 is not present in the array.

hints.text: Handle edge cases like empty array or target number greater than array size.

editorial.title: Binary Search Edge Case Error
editorial.intuition: Binary search works by finding the midpoint of the search interval and comparing the target value to the value at that index.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force approach would be to iterate over the array and return the index of the target value if found.
editorial.optimalSolution: If the target value is less than the middle element, repeat the search in the left half; otherwise, repeat the search in the right half.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: If the array is sorted, the target value will be present at the correct index if it's present, or greater than the middle element if not.
commonMistakes: Not handling edge cases like empty array or target number greater than array size.
Not returning -1 if the target value is not present in the array.

functionSignature.java: public boolean binarySearch(int[] nums, int target);
functionSignature.python: def binarySearch(self, nums: list[int], target: int) -> bool:
